# Feature: Web Expert Agent — Smart Retrieval

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-WEX-005
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Make the Web Expert Agent's retrieval pipeline notice more of what users are actually asking about. Today the `retrieveNode` ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:273-313](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L273-L313)) only:

- Detects RFC numbers via `RFC_PATTERN` ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:256](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L256)) and fetches the RFC.
- Maps a small list of keywords (~17 entries in `QUERY_CATEGORY_KEYWORDS` at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:235-253](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L235-L253)) to reference categories.
- Calls `webFetcher.search(query, categories)`.

It misses obvious signals: pasted URLs, three-digit HTTP status codes in prose, well-known header names, common topics like cookies/CORS/JWT/CSP. This feature broadens detection so the model gets more relevant grounding context per call.

This feature:

1. **Expands `QUERY_CATEGORY_KEYWORDS`** with ~30 additional common web terms.
2. **Detects HTTP status codes** in prose (e.g., "why did I get a 429?") and triggers RFC 9110 fetch.
3. **Detects well-known HTTP header names** (case-insensitive) and adds focused context.
4. **Detects pasted URLs** in the user query and triggers `webFetcher.fetchUrl()` (or equivalent) to ground on the actual content.
5. **Adds detection caps and dedup** so a single message doesn't trigger N+1 fetches.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (TypeScript strict, naming, async/await, semicolons, Vitest+RTL) are in [.spec-lite/memory.md](.spec-lite/memory.md). Only spec-specific notes below.

### Approach

- All edits land in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts) and possibly [packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts](packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts) if a generic URL-fetch method needs to be added.
- **`QUERY_CATEGORY_KEYWORDS`** ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:235-253](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L235-L253)) — extend with ~30 entries covering missing common topics.
- **Status-code detection** — add a regex constant `STATUS_CODE_PATTERN = /\b([1-5]\d{2})\b/g` plus a Set of valid IANA HTTP status codes (or a range check 100–599 with a curated allowlist of *named* codes). When detected, fetch the relevant section of RFC 9110.
- **Header-name detection** — maintain a static set of ~80 well-known HTTP header names (case-insensitive). Match against word boundaries in the query. When matched, append a category-focused context entry and increment the search categories.
- **URL detection** — add a regex `URL_PATTERN = /\bhttps?:\/\/[^\s)]+/g`. When matched, call `webFetcher.fetchUrl(url)` (extending the fetcher if needed) and add the fetched content to context. Honor the existing reference-website allowlist OR explicitly opt URL paste into a separate "user-provided URL" path with a higher trust threshold (decision below).
- **Detection caps**:
  - At most **3 RFCs** fetched per message (in case the user pastes a paragraph mentioning many RFCs).
  - At most **2 URLs** fetched per message.
  - At most **5 status codes** detected per message; coalesce into one fetch (RFC 9110 once).
  - Dedup all sources by URL before adding to `context`/`sources`.
- **Auto-fetch policy decision (URL paste)**: Two options:
  - (A) Fetch any pasted URL (broad but may follow user-controlled URLs to arbitrary content).
  - (B) Fetch only URLs whose origin matches the existing reference-website allowlist; otherwise note the URL in context with `Unverified:` framing (per FEAT-FP-WEX-003) and prompt the user to invoke `/rfc` or similar if it's a spec.
  - **Recommendation: (B)** — safer default, keeps fetcher rate-limit and allowlist contract intact. Document the policy in the JSDoc and revisit if users ask for (A).

### Spec-Specific Overrides

No overrides — see memory.

### Out of Scope

- Implementing the LLM-as-tool pattern (the model deciding when to fetch via tool calls). That is a separate, heavier change tracked in TODO.
- Vector-store / `'local'` mode (existing TODO; mode itself removed in FEAT-FP-WEX-004).
- Any prompt-side changes — this is a code-only feature. The prompt's MUST-fetch rules (added by FEAT-FP-WEX-003) cover *what the model should do*; this feature covers *what the routing layer detects automatically before the model runs*.
- Changing the reference-website allowlist or rate-limit configuration.

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

N/A — no persistent data model changes.

### In-Memory Constants Added

- `STATUS_CODE_PATTERN: RegExp` — global match.
- `KNOWN_STATUS_CODES: ReadonlySet<number>` — IANA-registered status codes.
- `URL_PATTERN: RegExp` — global match.
- `KNOWN_HEADERS: ReadonlySet<string>` — lowercased canonical names of common HTTP headers.

## 4. Files

- `packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts` — extend `QUERY_CATEGORY_KEYWORDS`, add detection constants and helpers, update `retrieveNode`.
- `packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts` — add `fetchUrl(url: string)` method if it doesn't exist (or expose existing internal fetch).
- `packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts` — add detection tests.
- `packages/arena/src/test/tools/webFetcher.test.ts` — add `fetchUrl()` test if the method is new.

## 5. Dependencies

- **None hard.** This feature is independent of FEAT-FP-WEX-001/002/003/004 — it touches retrieval logic only.
- **Soft sequencing**: shipping after FEAT-FP-WEX-003 is preferred so the prompt's MUST-fetch rules and the routing-layer detection logic are aligned in the same release. Shipping before is acceptable; the model just won't have the explicit prompt-side rules urging it to use the additional context.
- **Soft sequencing**: shipping after FEAT-FP-WEX-004 simplifies merge — both features touch `retrieveNode`. If FEAT-FP-WEX-004 ships first, this feature appends to the existing structured `commandHints` mechanism (recommended) instead of inventing a parallel one.

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Expand `QUERY_CATEGORY_KEYWORDS`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Edit `QUERY_CATEGORY_KEYWORDS` at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:235-253](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L235-L253). Add (and merge into existing entries where applicable):
    - `cookie: ['http', 'web']`
    - `cache: ['http', 'web']`
    - `etag: ['http', 'standards']`
    - `redirect: ['http', 'standards']`
    - `sse: ['protocols', 'web']`
    - `webhook: ['api', 'web']`
    - `jwt: ['standards', 'security']`
    - `pkce: ['standards', 'security']`
    - `oidc: ['standards', 'security']`
    - `csp: ['security', 'web']`
    - `hsts: ['security', 'standards']`
    - `cors: ['security', 'http', 'web']` (currently only `['http', 'web']` — add `security`)
    - `mime: ['http', 'standards']`
    - `multipart: ['http', 'standards']`
    - `proxy: ['http', 'standards']`
    - `cdn: ['http', 'web']`
    - `compression: ['http', 'standards']`
    - `hpack: ['protocols', 'standards']`
    - `qpack: ['protocols', 'standards']`
    - `mtls: ['security', 'standards']`
    - `webrtc: ['protocols', 'web']`
    - `webtransport: ['protocols', 'web']`
    - `mcp: ['protocols', 'api']`
    - `graphql: ['api', 'web']` (already exists — verify no regression)
    - `oauth: ['standards', 'security', 'web']` (currently `['standards', 'web']` — add `security`)
    - `tls: ['standards', 'protocols', 'security']` (currently `['standards', 'protocols']` — add `security`)
    - `crypto: ['web', 'standards', 'security']` (currently `['web', 'standards']` — add `security`)
  - Verify the `'security'` category is recognized by `webFetcher` — if not, this is a precondition flag, not a blocker (search will simply ignore an unknown category).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: update the inline JSDoc comment above the constant.
- **Verify**: build succeeds; existing tests still pass.

### TASK-002: Add status-code detection

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Add module-level constants in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts):
    - `STATUS_CODE_PATTERN: RegExp = /\b([1-5]\d{2})\b/g`
    - `KNOWN_STATUS_CODES: ReadonlySet<number>` — populated with the IANA HTTP status code registry (curate manually: 100, 101, 102, 103, 200, 201, 202, 203, 204, 205, 206, 207, 208, 226, 300, 301, 302, 303, 304, 305, 307, 308, 400, 401, 402, 403, 404, 405, 406, 407, 408, 409, 410, 411, 412, 413, 414, 415, 416, 417, 418, 421, 422, 423, 424, 425, 426, 428, 429, 431, 451, 500, 501, 502, 503, 504, 505, 506, 507, 508, 510, 511).
  - Add a helper `function detectStatusCodes(query: string): number[]` that returns up to 5 unique known status codes from the query (preserving order of first occurrence).
  - In `retrieveNode`, after the `RFC_PATTERN` block, call `detectStatusCodes(query)`. If any are found:
    - Coalesce into a single `webFetcher.fetchRfc('9110')` call (RFC 9110 covers HTTP semantics including status codes; section anchors are out of scope here — full RFC fetch is fine and dedup'd by URL).
    - Add a context entry: `"Status code(s) referenced: <comma-separated list>. See RFC 9110 §15."`
    - Avoid duplicate fetch if RFC 9110 was already fetched in this call.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `detectStatusCodes()`.
- **Verify**: build succeeds.

### TASK-003: Add header-name detection

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Add `KNOWN_HEADERS: ReadonlySet<string>` constant — lowercased canonical names. Curate from IANA Message Header Field Registry; aim for ~80 most-common (Accept, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language, Authorization, Cache-Control, Content-Type, Content-Length, Content-Encoding, Cookie, Set-Cookie, ETag, Expires, Host, If-Match, If-None-Match, Last-Modified, Location, Origin, Referer, User-Agent, Vary, WWW-Authenticate, X-Forwarded-For, Strict-Transport-Security, Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, Access-Control-Allow-Origin, Access-Control-Allow-Methods, Access-Control-Allow-Headers, Access-Control-Allow-Credentials, Access-Control-Expose-Headers, Access-Control-Max-Age, Access-Control-Request-Method, Access-Control-Request-Headers, Sec-Fetch-Mode, Sec-Fetch-Site, Sec-Fetch-Dest, Sec-Fetch-User, Sec-WebSocket-Key, Sec-WebSocket-Accept, Sec-WebSocket-Protocol, Sec-WebSocket-Version, Upgrade, Connection, Transfer-Encoding, Content-Disposition, Range, Accept-Ranges, Content-Range, Server, Date, Allow, Alt-Svc, Link, Permissions-Policy, Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy, Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy, Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy, Referrer-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Forwarded, Via, Retry-After, Age, Max-Forwards, Pragma, Warning, TE, Trailer, Expect, Accept-CH, DPR, Save-Data, Viewport-Width, Width, Early-Data, Priority).
  - Add helper `function detectHeaders(query: string): string[]` that scans the query (case-insensitive, word-boundary-anchored) for matches and returns up to 5 unique canonical header names.
  - In `retrieveNode`, call `detectHeaders(query)`. If found, add a context entry: `"HTTP header(s) referenced: <comma-separated>. Cite RFC and MDN."` and append `'http'` and `'web'` to the categories list passed to `webFetcher.search()`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `detectHeaders()`.
- **Verify**: build succeeds.

### TASK-004: Add URL detection and fetch

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Add `URL_PATTERN: RegExp = /\bhttps?:\/\/[^\s)]+/g` in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts).
  - Add helper `function detectUrls(query: string): string[]` returning up to 2 unique URLs.
  - In [packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts](packages/arena/src/tools/webFetcher.ts), add a method `fetchUrl(url: string): Promise<{ title: string; content: string; url: string }>`. Behavior:
    - Parse the URL; if origin is in the reference-website allowlist (existing config), fetch and parse normally.
    - If origin is NOT in the allowlist, return `{ title: '<external URL>', content: '', url }` without fetching, and log a `console.warn` indicating the policy. The caller (`retrieveNode`) then adds context: `"User pasted external URL <url>; not auto-fetched per allowlist policy. Ask the user if they want it fetched explicitly."`
  - In `retrieveNode`, after status-code/header detection, call `detectUrls(query)`. For each URL, call `webFetcher.fetchUrl()`. On success, append to `context` and `sources` (dedup'd).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-005, plus a unit test for `webFetcher.fetchUrl()` in [packages/arena/src/test/tools/webFetcher.test.ts](packages/arena/src/test/tools/webFetcher.test.ts) covering: allowlisted origin → fetched; non-allowlisted origin → not fetched, warn logged.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `detectUrls()` and `fetchUrl()` documenting the allowlist policy decision (option B).
- **Verify**: build succeeds; new fetcher test passes.

### TASK-005: Unit tests for all detection paths

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In [packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts](packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts), add a `describe('smart retrieval detection', ...)` block.
  - **Test 1 — Expanded keywords**: invoke `chat()` with `"how does CSP nonce-source work?"`. Spy on `webFetcher.search`. Assert called with categories including both `'security'` and `'web'`.
  - **Test 2 — Status-code detection**: invoke with `"why am I getting a 429 from this API?"`. Spy on `webFetcher.fetchRfc`. Assert called with `'9110'`. Assert context contains `"Status code(s) referenced: 429"`.
  - **Test 3 — Multiple status codes coalesce**: invoke with `"what's the difference between 401 and 403?"`. Assert `fetchRfc` called once with `'9110'` (not twice). Assert context lists both codes.
  - **Test 4 — Status-code dedup with explicit RFC**: invoke with `"per RFC 9110, when do I see a 451?"`. Assert `fetchRfc` called only once total (not once for the explicit RFC and again for the status code).
  - **Test 5 — Invalid status code excluded**: invoke with `"port 8080 is failing"`. Assert no status-code-driven fetch (8080 is not a 3-digit code; 080 is filtered by leading-zero / known-codes set).
  - **Test 6 — Header detection**: invoke with `"explain Strict-Transport-Security"`. Assert search categories include `'http'` and `'web'`. Assert context mentions the header.
  - **Test 7 — Header detection case-insensitive**: invoke with `"what does cache-control: no-store do?"`. Assert detection succeeds despite lowercase.
  - **Test 8 — URL detection allowlisted**: invoke with `"explain https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110"`. Assert `webFetcher.fetchUrl` called with that URL. Assert content appended to context.
  - **Test 9 — URL detection non-allowlisted**: invoke with `"explain https://random-blog.example.com/post"`. Assert `fetchUrl` called and returned no content; assert context entry mentions allowlist policy and asks for explicit confirmation.
  - **Test 10 — Detection caps**: invoke with a query containing 4 RFCs and 4 URLs. Assert `fetchRfc` called at most 3 times and `fetchUrl` called at most 2 times.
  - **Test 11 — Source dedup**: invoke with `"compare RFC 9110 to https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110"`. Assert sources array contains the RFC URL exactly once.
  - Reuse existing `makeMockLLM()` and `fetchSpy` infrastructure; add a `webFetcher` spy fixture if not already present from FEAT-FP-WEX-004.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: this task *is* the test task.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none beyond JSDoc on the new helpers.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena test webExpertAgent` passes; all eleven new tests appear green.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001 through TASK-004.

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: All new fetcher calls follow the existing pattern — wrap in `try/catch`, log `console.warn` (matching [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:291](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L291) and [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:304](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L304)), continue retrieval without the failed source. Memory Error Handling §3 (do not swallow silently) applies — every catch logs context.
- **Logging**: Add `console.info('[WebExpert/retrieve] detected status codes', { codes })` and similar for headers/URLs so the existing log-tail script (memory Logging §1) shows detection activity. Never log raw user query content beyond first 80 chars (matching the existing pattern at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:425](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L425)) to avoid leaking PII.
- **Security**:
  - URL-fetch policy is allowlist-by-default (option B above) — no SSRF risk via user-controlled URLs.
  - Status-code and header detection use anchored regexes with character-class restrictions; no injection into downstream fetch URLs.
  - Per memory Security §4 (sanitize external input), the fetched URL content is treated as untrusted — passed only into the LLM context, never executed.
- **Performance**:
  - Detection caps (3 RFCs, 2 URLs, 5 status codes coalesced) prevent runaway fetch fan-out per message.
  - Source dedup prevents fetching the same URL twice in one call.
  - Existing per-domain rate limiter ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:128](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L128)) provides the cross-call ceiling.
- **Backwards compatibility**: Per memory General §3, not required. New detection paths only add context — they do not remove or alter existing behavior. `webFetcher.fetchUrl()` is a new method; existing callers unchanged.
